Pixel-space diffusion generates images by directly denoising raw pixel values, whereas latent diffusion operates within a compressed latent space created by an autoencoder to significantly reduce computational costs. This efficiency allows latent diffusion models to achieve high-quality image generation with much faster training and inference times compared to their pixel-space counterparts.
